what_it_does: Acceptance test module for the generate-for-mockups engine core. It exercises config loading and reconfigurability, feedback-thread reading, shared annotation-store gathering, plan-mode dispatch safety, and fail-loud error paths by injecting a deterministic recording launcher instead of a real subprocess.
observations: The module is framed as proving four contract properties from its docstring: declared config, feedback rendering, plan-mode safety, and fail-loud behavior.
A _RecordingLauncher class substitutes the real AI runner, returning changed_files=[] and recording permission_mode so plan-mode safety can be asserted without network or subprocess side effects.
Tests write temporary configs and seed real feedback threads under gm.FEEDBACK_DIR, cleaning them up in finally blocks.
test_reads_annotations_both_levels verifies gathering comments from both the mockup base address and descendant element addresses in a shared annotation store.
The run() function executes all tests sequentially inside a single TemporaryDirectory rather than relying on an external test runner like pytest.
standouts: Strong contract-first docstring that maps each test to a specific engine guarantee.
Dual-source feedback coverage: both per-mockup .feedback files and the shared annotations.jsonl store.
Deterministic launcher injection lets the acceptance suite run without a real Claude -p session.
conventions: Uses pytest-style test_* functions but also provides a manual run() entry point.
Follows a seed/cleanup pattern with tempfile and os.remove in finally blocks.
Prints human-readable progress markers such as [1] config declared + reconfigurable.
Prefers fail-loud assertions over silent no-ops.
concerns: Tests depend on the real gm.MOCKUPS_DIR and gm.FEEDBACK_DIR, so they are not fully hermetic.
test_reads_annotations_both_levels hardcodes the mockup 'C1-inbox-desktop.html' and assumes it maps to ui://inbox, which could break if corpus metadata changes.
run() reuses one TemporaryDirectory across all tests, so leftover files from one test could influence another if cleanup is missed.
_RecordingLauncher.__call__ signature may not exactly match the real runner contract expected by gm.generate_for_mockup.
notes: Acceptance harness that trades full hermeticity for realistic config/feedback verification by injecting only the subprocess launcher.
questions: Is this suite intended to run under pytest, as a standalone script, or both?
What is the exact signature and behavior expected of the launcher passed to gm.generate_for_mockup?
How does the engine map a mockup filename like C1-inbox-desktop.html to a ui://inbox address?
Where is the separate real round-trip with claude -p verified, and how is it kept in sync with this deterministic suite?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—proving four contract properties of the generate-for-mockups engine core—matches the actual tests, with additional coverage for annotation-store gathering and config error paths.
intention_signals: The file is trying to provide a fast, deterministic acceptance gate that documents and enforces the engine's public contract—especially that behavior is driven by declared config and feedback, that plan mode never mutates files, and that missing or invalid inputs raise rather than silently no-op.
novelty: Injecting a recording launcher to acceptably stub the expensive AI subprocess while still exercising real config parsing, template rendering, feedback reading, and annotation-store union logic.
contribution: A runnable, contract-oriented acceptance harness that captures the expected invariants of the generate-mockup engine and can be reused as a regression guard during engine evolution.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance tests for the generate-for-mockups engine core that inject a deterministic launcher to verify config reconfigurability, feedback and annotation rendering, plan-mode non-mutation, and fail-loud error handling.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a fast, deterministic acceptance suite proving the generate-mockup engine's contract without invoking a real Claude subprocess.

symbols.does: Returns the first .html file found in gm.MOCKUPS_DIR.
symbols.description: Utility that selects an existing mockup file on disk so tests can exercise the engine against a real mockup without hardcoding a filename in most cases. It asserts that at least one mockup exists, failing fast if the directory is empty.

symbols.does: Writes a temporary generate-config.json with optional overrides.
symbols.description: Creates an isolated config file inside the provided temp directory, starting from a baseline plan-mode template and routing setup. Supports reconfigurability tests by letting callers override values such as the instruction_template without mutating the canonical design config.

symbols.does: Writes a newline-delimited JSON feedback thread for a mockup.
symbols.description: Creates a .feedback file for the given mockup under gm.FEEDBACK_DIR using a dedicated test-named thread. Each note is serialized as JSON with id, mockup, element, text, timestamp, and status, enabling tests to assert that the engine reads and renders captured feedback.

symbols.does: Deterministic stand-in for the real AI runner.
symbols.description: Class implementing the runner contract expected by the engine's launch layer. It records the rendered instruction and permission_mode, then returns a structured plan-mode result with changed_files=[], so acceptance tests can verify dispatch and safety without spawning a real subprocess.
Sets up an empty list used to capture every invocation of the launcher, allowing tests to inspect the instruction and permission_mode passed by the engine.

symbols.does: Records an engine launch and returns a fake plan-mode result.
symbols.description: Accepts the instruction, repo, permission_mode, and timeout arguments, appends them to the call log, and returns a result dict indicating success with no changed files. This models a safe plan-mode run and exposes the permission_mode for safety assertions.

symbols.does: Verifies that config values are read and can be changed.
symbols.description: Writes two configs that differ only in instruction_template, runs the engine with each, and asserts that the rendered instruction reflects ALPHA-TEMPLATE versus BETA-TEMPLATE. Proves the engine uses declared config data and that behavior changes when config changes without code edits.

symbols.does: Verifies feedback notes are read and rendered into the instruction.
symbols.description: Seeds two feedback notes with element loci, runs the engine in plan mode with an empty annotation store root, and asserts that both note texts and the element address appear in the rendered instruction_core and that the actionable feedback count is 2.

symbols.does: Verifies plan mode dispatches but commits no changes.
symbols.description: Runs the engine in plan mode with a recording launcher and asserts that a dispatch occurred with permission_mode='plan', changed_files is empty, a proposed summary is returned, and the instruction includes the configured scope line.

symbols.does: Verifies the engine raises when no actionable feedback exists.
symbols.description: Removes any existing feedback thread for a real mockup and points the annotation store at an empty temp directory, then asserts that gm.generate_for_mockup raises a GenerateError containing 'no actionable feedback'.

symbols.does: Verifies gathering of whole-mockup and element-level annotations.
symbols.description: Creates a shared annotations.jsonl store with comments at ui://inbox and ui://inbox/lane plus an unrelated ui://settings entry. Asserts gather_feedback returns only the inbox-related comments and that generate_for_mockup renders them with an actionable count of 2.

symbols.does: Verifies the engine raises on an unknown mockup file.
symbols.description: Attempts to generate for a non-existent mockup filename and asserts that gm.generate_for_mockup raises a GenerateError containing 'unknown mockup'.

symbols.does: Verifies load_config raises on missing or malformed config files.
symbols.description: First attempts to load a non-existent config path, then writes invalid JSON to a config file and attempts to load it, asserting that both cases raise gm.GenerateError.

symbols.does: Executes all acceptance tests in sequence.
symbols.description: Creates a single TemporaryDirectory, runs each test function with it, prints progress markers, and prints an ALL GREEN message on completion. Serves as a standalone entry point when the module is run as a script.
